results.email: Hi, I was charged $49.99 twice this month for my Pro subscription. Can you refund the duplicate charge? Order confirmation numbers are #4471 and #4472, both dated the 3rd.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports a duplicate subscription charge and requests a refund.
results.actual_summary: Customer was charged twice ($49.99 each) for their Pro subscription on the 3rd and is requesting a refund for the duplicate charge (Order #4471 and #4472).
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue (duplicate subscription charge and refund request) as the reference, and includes additional accurate details from the email without contradicting anything.

results.email: why does my invoice say net 30 but the portal shows overdue after 15 days??? this is the second time this has happened
results.expected_summary: Customer flags a discrepancy between stated and enforced invoice payment terms.
results.actual_summary: Customer is disputing a discrepancy where their invoice states Net 30 payment terms but the portal incorrectly marks the account as overdue after only 15 days, and this is a recurring issue.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue as the reference — a discrepancy between stated invoice payment terms (Net 30) and enforced terms (overdue after 15 days) — and adds accurate supporting details from the email without misrepresenting anything.

results.email: Every time I try to export my report as a PDF the app just freezes on 'Generating...' and I have to force-quit. Happens on both Chrome and Safari, macOS 14.5.
results.expected_summary: Customer's PDF export feature freezes the app across multiple browsers.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing an app freeze when attempting to export a report as a PDF, occurring on both Chrome and Safari browsers on macOS 14.5.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue (app freezes during PDF export across multiple browsers) and adds minor details (macOS 14.5, specific browser names) that are consistent with and supported by the original email, without missing or contradicting any key information.

results.expected_summary: Customer reports the app is not working, with no further detail provided.
results.actual_summary: Customer is reporting that the app is broken.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference — the customer is reporting that the app is not working/broken — with no missing or incorrect details.

results.email: I reset my password twice using the email link but it still says incorrect password when I try to log in. Locked out for two days now.
results.expected_summary: Customer remains locked out of their account despite resetting their password twice.
results.actual_summary: Customer has been locked out of their account for two days despite resetting their password twice via email link, as the system still shows an incorrect password error.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference (locked out despite two password resets), and the additional details (two days, email link, incorrect password error) are accurate elaborations from the original email, not contradictions or misstatements.

results.email: can u chnage the email on my acount from old@gmial.com to new@gmail.com thanksss
results.expected_summary: Customer requests to change the email address associated with their account.
results.actual_summary: Customer is requesting to update the email address on their account from old@gmial.com to new@gmail.com.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core request as the reference (changing the email address on the account) and additionally includes the specific email addresses, which is consistent with the original email and does not contradict the reference.

results.email: Love the new dashboard redesign, way easier to find what I need now. Any plans to add dark mode?
results.expected_summary: Customer gives positive feedback on the dashboard redesign and asks about dark mode.
results.actual_summary: Customer compliments the new dashboard redesign and inquires about plans to add a dark mode feature.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference: positive feedback on the dashboard redesign and a question about dark mode, just expressed with slightly different but equivalent wording.

results.email: Oh great, ANOTHER update that changes where the export button is. Really appreciate having to relearn your UI every month. Fantastic work as always.
results.expected_summary: Customer sarcastically criticizes the frequency of UI changes.
results.actual_summary: Customer is frustrated that a recent UI update moved the location of the export button, forcing them to relearn the interface.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate correctly captures the customer's frustration about UI changes, but focuses on the specific export button detail while missing the reference's emphasis on the *frequency* of changes (sarcasm about "every month"), which is the core point of the complaint.

results.email: Hola, mi factura de este mes es incorrecta, me cobraron por 3 usuarios pero solo tenemos 2 licencias activas.
results.expected_summary: Customer says this month's invoice incorrectly charged for 3 users instead of 2.
results.actual_summary: The customer reports an incorrect charge on their current invoice, as they were billed for 3 users but only have 2 active licenses.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference: incorrect invoice charge for 3 users when only 2 licenses/users are active, with no missing or incorrect details.

results.email: API returning 500 errors intermittently on POST /v2/orders since ~14:00 UTC today. About 1 in 5 requests. Retrying doesn't help. Need urgent help, this is affecting our checkout flow in production.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports intermittent 500 errors on a production API endpoint affecting checkout.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing intermittent 500 errors on POST /v2/orders since ~14:00 UTC, affecting 1 in 5 requests and disrupting their production checkout flow.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the core information in the reference (intermittent 500 errors, production API endpoint, checkout affected) and adds additional specific details from the original email without contradicting anything.

results.email: This is the third email I'm sending. I need my account deleted and all my data removed per GDPR. Please confirm within 30 days as required by law.
results.expected_summary: Customer requests account deletion and data removal under GDPR, noting repeated prior contact.
results.actual_summary: The customer is urgently requesting account deletion and full data removal in compliance with GDPR, and expects confirmation within the legally required 30-day period.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate captures the core GDPR account deletion and data removal request, but omits the detail about repeated prior contact (third email), which is a minor but relevant secondary detail present in the reference.

results.email: Do you offer volume discounts for teams over 200 seats? Would like to talk to sales.
results.expected_summary: Prospective customer asks about volume discounts and requests to speak with sales.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about volume discounts for teams over 200 seats and would like to be connected with the sales team.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures both core elements from the reference: the inquiry about volume discounts and the request to speak with sales, while adding the specific detail about "200 seats" which is consistent with the original email and doesn't contradict the reference.

results.email: not sure if this is a bug or if im just dumb but the search bar doesnt find anything even when i type the exact title of a document i KNOW exists
results.expected_summary: Customer reports that search fails to find documents even with exact title matches.
results.actual_summary: Customer is reporting that the search bar returns no results even when searching for the exact title of a known existing document.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue as the reference: the search bar failing to find documents even when the exact title is used, with no missing or incorrect information.

results.email: downgrade me to the free plan effective immediately, and I want a prorated refund for the unused days on my current plan
results.expected_summary: Customer requests an immediate downgrade to the free plan and a prorated refund.
results.actual_summary: Customer wants to immediately downgrade to the free plan and receive a prorated refund for the unused days remaining on their current paid plan.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference—immediate downgrade to the free plan and a prorated refund—with the additional detail about "unused days remaining on their current paid plan" that is consistent with the original email and doesn't introduce any inaccuracies.

results.expected_summary: Customer's message consists only of '2fa', with no further context provided.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about two-factor authentication (2FA).
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary incorrectly infers that the customer is "inquiring about" 2FA, while the reference makes clear that the message consists only of the text '2fa' with no further context — meaning no actual inquiry or intent can be determined.

results.email: Hey! Are you guys hiring? I'm a huge fan of the product and would love to work there.
results.expected_summary: Customer expresses interest in job opportunities at the company.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about job opportunities at the company, expressing enthusiasm for the product and interest in working there.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference — interest in job opportunities — and additionally includes the enthusiasm for the product, which is accurate and not contradictory.

results.email: getting a weird error, screenshot attached: 'Error 0x8007045D: cannot sync'. tried restarting, tried reinstalling, tried a different network. nothing works. this started right after your update yesterday.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports a persistent sync error that began after a recent update, despite multiple troubleshooting attempts.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing a persistent sync error (Error 0x8007045D) that began after a recent update, and has already attempted restarting, reinstalling, and switching networks without success.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference — persistent sync error, triggered by a recent update, with multiple failed troubleshooting attempts — and simply adds specific details (the error code and the exact steps tried) that are present in the original email, without introducing any incorrect information.

results.email: I cancelled my subscription last week but I can still see I'm being billed. Also separately, is there a way to get an itemized receipt for tax purposes?
results.expected_summary: Customer reports being billed after cancellation and asks about obtaining an itemized receipt.
results.actual_summary: Customer is being charged despite cancelling their subscription last week, and is also requesting an itemized receipt for tax purposes.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures both core issues: the billing after cancellation and the request for an itemized receipt, with the same meaning as the reference, just with slightly more detail (mentioning "last week" and "for tax purposes") that doesn't contradict anything.
